task:
Implement the bounded follow-up task supernote-cloud-ledger.cached-full-text. Add a `--include-text` option to the existing `supernote changes <notebook> --latest` CLI path, pass it into LatestNotebookStateRequest(include_text=True), keep default latest output preview-only, and add/update focused offline tests proving full cached OCR is returned without constructing SupernoteUploader. Update README.md and docs/supernote-cloud-change-ledger-contracts.md to document `supernote changes <notebook> --latest --include-text --json` as the full cached page-read command. Do not touch write/Cloud mutation paths, uv.lock, scratch, organizer, or commit. Run the task validation commands and return files/tests.

---
**Output:**
Write your findings to exactly this path: /tmp/supernote-full-text-worker.json
This path is authoritative for this run.
Ignore any other output filename or output path mentioned elsewhere, including output destinations in the base agent prompt, system prompt, or task instructions.

criteria.must: Implement the requested change without widening scope
Implement the requested change without widening scope

criteriaSatisfied.evidence: Added only the --include-text read-path wiring + guard + 2 focused offline tests + docs across the 5 task-scope files. The read contract was unchanged. No write/Cloud mutation paths, scratch, organizer, uv.lock, or commit touched; no files staged.

commandsRun.command: uv run ruff check src/paia_supernote/cli.py tests/test_agent_read_contracts.py tests/integration/test_agent_contract_surfaces.py
runtime smoke via real cli.main(): default --latest preview-only; --latest --include-text returns full OCR; --include-text without --latest rejected

validationOutput: supernote changes --help shows [--latest] [--include-text] with correct help text
--latest --include-text --json: pages[].text == full cached OCR, ocr_status ready, no Cloud contact
git diff --cached empty; git status clean except the 5 intended files + pre-existing uv.lock M
residualRisks: Prose (non --json) format_changes output still renders only text_preview for the pages branch; the full text is intentionally exposed via --json as documented (full cached page-read is specified as a --json command). Out of scope per the bounded task.
childReport.diffSummary: Wired a --include-text boolean through the changes --latest read path into LatestNotebookStateRequest(include_text=...), added a requires-latest guard, and covered it with one offline unit test (uploader-fail monkeypatch proving full OCR + preview-only default + guard) and one integration CLI-surface test; updated README and the ledger contract doc to document the exact full cached page-read command. No contract/mutation code changed.
reviewFindings: no blockers. Guard `--include-text requires --latest` follows the existing `--advance requires --agent` precedent; default preview-only behavior is asserted in both tests and at runtime.
note: uv.lock is M in git but was modified before this session (mtime predates session edits); uv run did not alter it.
childReport.manualNotes: Runtime mtime check confirms uv.lock not touched this session. The read contract file (agent_read_contracts.py) already implemented include_text and preview-by-default, so it intentionally required zero edits. Output written to /tmp/supernote-full-text-worker.json.
